subject: I'm sure you've heard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Friend, by now you've probably heard about our One-Year-Out fundraising goal. Well, the deadline is tonight at midnight, and Samantha tells me we still need 231 supporters to step up.

We set an ambitious goal of 1,000 contributions over a 48-hour time period because it is so important for us to keep building our grassroots momentum. Now our deadline is just a few hours away. body:
| Friend --

What a debate!

It takes a lot to build a history of being a true conservative while winning in a blue state, and I'm proud to have taken the debate stage tonight as the only candidate to have both on his resume. While other candidates bickered, I continued to stand and fight for the American worker.

That's how I won tonight's debate.

And the folks out there on social media agreed. **[POLITICO tonight declared me the winner of the debate as the one who sparked the most conversation.](http://www.ricksantorum.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.politico.com%2Fblogs%2Flive-from-milwaukee%2F2015%2F11%2Ftwitter-winner-undercard-debate-rick-santorum-215727&e=84b355b85f9e9360b9dac4e2a50bbee5&utm_source=santorum&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20151110_debate&n=1)** That's why I keep going -- Americans need to know that there's a candidate out there standing for their traditional values, fighting to rebuild the economy, and strengthen our stature overseas.

Let's keep the momentum going! **[Donating to my campaign will make sure that we can keep up the fight for American families going strong.](http://www.ricksantorum.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fcauses.anedot.com%2Fricksantorum%3Fsc%3D20151110-debate&e=84b355b85f9e9360b9dac4e2a50bbee5&utm_source=santorum&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20151110_debate&n=2)**

Thank you!

-Rick

name: Rand Paul
subject: Video message from Rand
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
The reviews are in and last night was a resounding success.
The moderators did a good job and I had
enough time to get my message out.

Analysts across the country are saying this
was a big moment for our campaign. Even the New York Times said I
won the debate. We won several polls including the Fox News Poll.

I took on Marco Rubio for his free spending
policies that would add $2 Trillion to our national debt. I
pointed out that I want a strong military, but it is hard to have
a strong military if the country goes bankrupt.

It turns out that standing for liberty and
real conservative principles does resonate with people. We're all
tired of the politicians going from talking point to talking
point with their next big interview in mind. I could care less
about that sort of thing -- I went to the debate to prove a point
about the recklessness of Washington's failed spending policies.

I took on debt, the Fed, and spending
trillions of dollars that we don't have, and how that will put us
even further in debt to countries that don't like us.

Now that they are saying we won the debate we
need to take action and make sure that we can capitalize on the
momentum.

To celebrate, my team put together a DEBATE
VICTORY MONEY BOMB. I hope you can chip in and help.

$10 Money Bomb >>

$25 Money Bomb >>

$50 Money Bomb >>

$100 Money Bomb >>

Or donate another
amount >>

Your donation of any size would make a big
difference.

This debate victory is showing finally
proving that standing up for liberty is popular with the voters.
Americans know that solutions to our country's problems come from
following the Constitution and the Bill of Rights.

Will you stand with me once again?

< < Pitch in now to
our victory money bomb > >

In Liberty,

Rand

Thanks you for your support.

-John

name: Lindsey Graham
subject: Honoring their Service
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lindsey
body:
LindseyGraham.com

All gave some, some gave all. I will honor our veterans'
sacrifice.

Friend,

Today we honor the enormous sacrifices of our veterans. The men
and women of the U.S. military don't simply do a job, they commit
their lives. They go wherever they are needed, whether that takes
them to the front lines of battle or to lonely outposts to
perform vital support work. They put unfathomable strains on
their physical and mental health, and they often do not return
home whole. They miss out not only on birthdays and anniversaries
but on the quiet, daily comforts of being home with their loved
ones. Their entire families must continually start over in new
neighborhoods and new schools, with their spouses often bearing
the brunt of these challenges on their own. These are enormous
weights that we ask our veterans and their families to bear, and
I have seen first-hand the mixture of dedication and stress with
which they bear them.

While it is a debt we can't fully repay, there are ways that we
can and absolutely must pay back what we owe our veterans. First
and foremost, we need a commander in chief who will always give
our service men and women the support they need to succeed and
never squander the gains they have achieved.

President Barack Obama has failed our veterans on both fronts.
Here at home, he has presided over defense cuts that are gutting
our military's capability, capacity, and readiness. Military
equipment is aging and falling behind technologically. Troops are
not receiving the full training they need to prepare for what
lies ahead. Our overwhelming global superiority is deeply
threatened, and yet President Obama had the gall to veto this
year's defense funding authorization bill for purely political
reasons.

Even more disturbing are President Obama's actions overseas that
are squandering the hard-fought gains our troops have already
made and jeopardizing their ability to succeed in the future. Our
men and women in uniform did their jobs in Iraq and did them
admirably. The Surge worked. It stemmed the violence, made common
cause with Sunni leadership, and created space for political
progress. This tremendous success came at a steep price,
including thousands of American lives and tens of thousands of
American wounded. Our service men and women did their duty
regardless of the costs because they had seen the enemy up close
and understood that we must take the fight to them before they
attack us again here at home. President Obama, however, failed to
understand this, ignored the advice of his military commanders,
and risked it all with his total withdrawal from Iraq in 2011.

He has compounded this disastrous decision - which created
the conditions that have allowed the rise of ISIL and the
resurgence of Iranian influence in Iraq and throughout the
region - with one failure after another. He refused to stand
with the Syrian people when they rose up against President Assad,
refused to assist the Free Syrian Army when it could have made a
difference in the fight against both Assad and ISIL, and he even
refused to enforce his own red line on Assad's use of chemical
weapons.

He has incrementally sent U.S. troops into the region, first in
Iraq and now a small handful of special forces into Syria, while
failing to develop an overall strategy that these troops can
fight to achieve. All the while, he has embarked on quixotic
efforts to appease and cajole the Ayatollah of Iran,
strengthening this religious Nazi's position regionally at the
expense of Israel and our Arab allies. The net effect of these
repeated blunders has been to threaten the gains of our military,
both past and future. They make a mockery of the sacrifices of
our veterans and their families. Our next commander in chief will
have to ask our men and women in uniform to correct these
failures and re-take that ground.

I am running for president because I have an unshakeable
commitment to these brave men and women. I wore the uniform for
over 30 years. I spent more than six years on active duty,
including several years deployed to Germany. I've made dozens of
trips to Iraq and Afghanistan as a reservist and a Senator. I
understand that as president there is no greater responsibility
than to be a worthy and successful commander in chief. I will
never send a single man or woman into harm's way without the
strategy and resources to succeed. And I will never squander the
gains they make at great personal cost.

I also will never break faith with those who return from the
fight bearing the scars of service. Every day that a single man
or woman who has suffered in service to our country goes without
proper care is a national disgrace. I have been
fighting - and will continue to fight each day - to
ensure that every single veteran receives timely, quality care.
This includes reforming a broken VA system, but I believe we must
go much further by providing access for every veteran to private
health care outside of the VA. If the VA isn't working for you,
you should be able to go to another facility that will. Increased
access will improve the lives of veterans, and increased
competition will ultimately strengthen the VA system itself and
ensure its long-term viability. I won't rest until all our
veterans receive the care they need, when they need it.

To the American people, I will fight every day to keep this
nation safe. To the men and women of the military - both
those currently fighting and those who have previously worn the
uniform - I will fight every day to honor your service and be
worthy of the enormous sacrifices you make.

Sincerely,

Lindsey Graham

This note originally appeared on Medium.com on Wednesday,
November 11, 2015.

Paid for by Lindsey
Graham 2016

This includes reforming a broken VA system, but I believe we must go much further by providing access for every veteran to private health care outside of the VA. If the VA isn't working for you, you should be able to go to another facility that will. Increased access will improve the lives of veterans, and increased competition will ultimately strengthen the VA system itself and ensure its long-term viability. I won't rest until all our veterans receive the care they need, when they need it. To the American people, I will fight every day to keep this nation safe. To the men and women of the military - both those currently fighting and those who have previously worn the uniform - I will fight every day to honor your service and be worthy of the enormous sacrifices you make. Sincerely, Lindsey Graham This note originally appeared on Medium.com on Wednesday, November 11, 2015. name: Katie McGinty
subject: They served us. Now, it's our turn.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate |
body:
Friend,

The brave men and women of our armed forces serve our country with honor and courage. They protect us both here and abroad, and they fight every day to preserve the freedoms we hold so dear.

So when they conclude their service, when they return home to their communities and their families, the rest of us have an obligation to serve them. Today we celebrate Veterans Day, a day when we should not only honor the brave sacrifices made by our veterans, but also reaffirm our commitment to serving them when they come home.

Add your name here to join me in thanking our veterans for their service.

https://act.myngp.com/Forms/-7052917391927279616?ms=20151111

Our veterans deserve the best America has to offer. They deserve jobs and training to help them transition to civilian life and use their skills in the marketplace. They deserve health care to help them recover from injuries sustained in service to their country. They deserve benefits and assistance and opportunities to pursue the American Dream, whether by opening their own small business or returning to school to pursue their education. I will always stand up for our veterans and advocate for policies that give them the honor and respect they deserve.

This Veterans Day, I am reaffirming my solemn commitment to serving our veterans as fiercely as they have served us.

Will you join me? Add your name to thank our veterans for their service to our country.

https://act.myngp.com/Forms/-7052917391927279616?ms=20151111

To all the veterans both here in Pennsylvania and throughout our country,I thank you. Thank you for your commitment. Thank you for your sacrifice. Thank you for your service.

Katie

name: Rand Paul
subject: Check Out These Headlines!
body:
Dear ,
"Rand Went on the Offensive, Reshaped the
Debate And Had His Strongest Performance Yet." -- Huffington Post
"Finally the Biggest Rift in the Republican
Party Has Been Exposed." -- Washington Post
"Rand Paul Shuts Down Donald Trump, Debate
Moderators, and Commercial Breaks." -- Vanity Fair
"Fireworks Erupt Between Marco Rubio and Rand
Paul on Military Spending" -- Business Insider
"Rand Paul Drops the Mic on Donald Trump Over
China's Participation in Trade Deal" -- Los Angeles Times
"Why Rand Paul Was the Stealth Winner of the
GOP Debate" -- The Week Magazine
"Rand Paul had best debate of the campaign."
-- Chuck Todd
"The reviews are in and it looks like Rand
Paul had a good night. Libertarians say he hit his stride. It was
clearly his finest performance of the four debates." -- Matt
Lewis, Daily Caller
Those are just a few headlines Americans woke
up to this morning describing my performance during last night's
Republican debate in Milwaukee.
, the battle lines between the Big
Government establishment and my campaign have now been drawn.

And for daring to point out the fact that we
don't have trillions of dollars to spend on Senator Rubio's new
welfare plans or pour into misguided military spending, I'm
already being attacked.
That's why I'm counting on you to stand with
me by giving as generously as you can to my Debate Victory Money
Bomb right away!
The truth is, last night's was the Big
Government establishment's worst nightmare. During the debate:
>>> I explained why income inequality is
worse where Democrats are in charge and how the Federal Reserve
allows the well-connected to live high on the hog while the rest
of us see the value of our money slowly evaporate;
>>> I explained why we need to get rid of
President Obama's outrageous environmental controls to get our
economy moving, and how Hillary Clinton's misguided foreign
policy is really no different than several of my opponents;
>>> I showed why my 14.5% flat-tax plan would
give every American a tax cut, create jobs and return $2 TRILLION
to the American people over ten years.
But exposing Senator Rubio's outlandish
spending plans is really what has the Big Government
establishment up in arms today.
Their big hope has been, with Senator Rubio,
they'll be able to put a new face on failed old Big Government
policies of the past.
Like Hillary Clinton, he's supported amnesty.
Like Hillary Clinton, he's supported arming Syrian rebels,
strengthening ISIS. He's supported toppling Gaddafi in Libya,
which strengthened radical Islamists.
Yet, even with our nation $18.5 TRILLION in
debt, no one on stage last night dared question if we can afford
another two trillion dollars in Congressional spending!
That's how strong the Washington machine's
grip is on so many running for our Republican party's
presidential nomination.
But the fact is, we can't continue to spend
like there's no tomorrow on anything.
Pretending we can only makes us less safe.

The truth is, there's no way to defend this
nation from bankruptcy court.
Yet even in my own Republican Party, there
are those who refuse to come to grips with our country's fiscal
reality.
No one but me was willing to tell the
American people the truth.
, we need a new direction. We need to
destroy the Washington machine that's bankrupting us.
Last night, I showed why I'm the only fiscal
conservative in the race.
But I'm counting on your most generous
contribution of $50, $25 or even $10 to support my Debate Victory
Money Bomb.

If we can reach our $500,000 goal, I believe
it will send a massive message to the media and the Big
Government establishment that their days are numbered.
So please act at once.
In liberty,
Rand

name: Eric Greitens
subject: How should we honor our veterans?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GREITENS FOR MISSOURI
body:
Peter,
When I came home after serving four tours as a Navy
SEAL, I saw that my friends and fellow veterans still had more to
give to our country. I realized that service for our servicemen
and women doesn't end when they leave the battlefield; it
continues when they return home. My message to friends who were
struggling post-war was very clear, "we still need you."
Those are more than just four simple words - they
inspired a mission. I created an organization, The Mission
Continues, that helps fellow veterans find ways to continue to
serve, allowing them to live a life of dignity, purpose, and
meaning when they return home. I was honored to join Patriot
radio on SiriusXM this morning to discuss the importance of
continuing to serve. (link to interview)
People often ask me, how should we honor our veterans?
The simple answer: live their values. Follow their example of
service and sacrifice. The greatest way that we can honor our
veterans, is by committing ourselves to the hard work of building
stronger families, stronger communities, and a stronger country.
On this Veterans Day, let's thank our veterans for
their service, and let's also recognize how much they still have
to give to our families, our communities, and our country.
Happy Veterans Day!
Best,
Eric
PAID FOR BY GREITENS FOR MISSOURI
JEFF STUERMAN, TREASURER

| | Last night at the GOP debate, I talked about a choice voters have to make. It’s obvious Republicans must stop Hillary Clinton from becoming President. But it’s not enough to put any Republican in the White House. We have seen what happens when we send big government Republicans to DC. They end up spending a little less than the Democrats would.

Here’s why this matters:

[](http://bobbyjindal.us12.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=87653a3e8fa722737340ef6fd&id=66e87747ef&e=c688ac0b49)

That’s what we need to do with the federal government.

I’m the only one running that has actually cut government spending. The only one. name: Bernie Sanders
subject: How we care for our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
#
Friend -
If you watched last night's debate, there was a lot of talk about war in places like Iraq and Syria, but very little about how to care for the men and women who serve after they return home.
**Today is Veterans Day -- a fact that went unmentioned during the Republican debate.** And that's important, because the truth is that while planes and tanks and guns are a cost of war, so too is taking care of the service members who use those weapons and fight our battles.
Last year, as chairman of the Veterans' Affairs Committee, I authored and passed the most comprehensive veterans' legislation in decades, reaching across the aisle to team up with Sen. John McCain. Amid reports of unacceptable wait times and calls to dangerously privatize veterans health care, we actually authorized funding for 27 new medical facilities and hired more doctors and nurses to care for the surging number of veterans returning from wars in Iraq and Afghanistan.
After two wars over 14 years, never before have so few been asked to do so much for our country. **I voted against the Iraq War, which I think will go down as one of the worst foreign policy blunders we have ever seen, but I have never wavered from my commitment to caring for the women and men who served, and continue to serve, in that conflict.**
You may not have heard much about it from the Republican candidates last night, but **how we care for our veterans is going to be a central issue this election.** The Koch-connected Concerned Veterans for America is prepared to spend untold millions of dollars **supporting the privatization of veterans ' health care.** And almost every Republican candidate running for president supports their plan to place the profits of private corporations over the promise made to our veterans.
I believe we should take a different approach -- that we should stand with the majority of veterans who believe we should continue strengthening the VA. Now I want to know that you're with us.
[**Sign my petition if I can count on you to help me continue fighting against the Koch-connected plans to privatize veterans ' health care.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2756/43f17890/53c89efa/621e9f38/44900098/VEsH/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/veterans-healthcare?source=em151111full")
This issue is very important to me and it's why I am so happy to receive so many letters from veterans who appreciate my work on their behalf.
People like Hilary from Polk County, Iowa who wrote to our campaign saying, "Bernie Sanders is the only candidate with a track record of fighting for veterans and veterans' rights. I know as president he won't send my brothers and sisters in arms into needless wars and for those that have served our country, he will ensure that they have access to the benefits and health care they earned through their service."
And Peter from San Diego, "Retired Navy. Like [Bernie's] stand on taking care of veterans. If you can't afford to take care of veterans you can't afford to fight a war."
And also Jack from Massachusetts, "I'm a disabled Marine combat veteran, Bernie has always supported veterans with deeds and not just hot air. I'd love to have a President like that."
I will always fight for Hilary, Peter, and Jack. And if we all stand together, we can protect and strengthen the care we provide for everyone who has served our country.
[**Sign my petition opposing Republican plans to privatize veterans ' health care.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2756/43f17890/53c89efa/621e9f38/44900098/VEsE/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/veterans-healthcare?source=em151111full")

clean_body: Friend - If you watched last night's debate, there was a lot of talk about war in places like Iraq and Syria, but very little about how to care for the men and women who serve after they return home. Today is Veterans Day -- a fact that went unmentioned during the Republican debate. And that's important, because the truth is that while planes and tanks and guns are a cost of war, so too is taking care of the service members who use those weapons and fight our battles. Last year, as chairman of the Veterans' Affairs Committee, I authored and passed the most comprehensive veterans' legislation in decades, reaching across the aisle to team up with Sen. John McCain. Amid reports of unacceptable wait times and calls to dangerously privatize veterans health care, we actually authorized funding for 27 new medical facilities and hired more doctors and nurses to care for the surging number of veterans returning from wars in Iraq and Afghanistan. After two wars over 14 years, never before have so few been asked to do so much for our country. I voted against the Iraq War, which I think will go down as one of the worst foreign policy blunders we have ever seen, but I have never wavered from my commitment to caring for the women and men who served, and continue to serve, in that conflict. You may not have heard much about it from the Republican candidates last night, but how we care for our veterans is going to be a central issue this election. The Koch-connected Concerned Veterans for America is prepared to spend untold millions of dollars supporting the privatization of veterans ' health care. And almost every Republican candidate running for president supports their plan to place the profits of private corporations over the promise made to our veterans. I believe we should take a different approach -- that we should stand with the majority of veterans who believe we should continue strengthening the VA. Now I want to know that you're with us. "" This issue is very important to me and it's why I am so happy to receive so many letters from veterans who appreciate my work on their behalf. People like Hilary from Polk County, Iowa who wrote to our campaign saying, "Bernie Sanders is the only candidate with a track record of fighting for veterans and veterans' rights. I know as president he won't send my brothers and sisters in arms into needless wars and for those that have served our country, he will ensure that they have access to the benefits and health care they earned through their service." And Peter from San Diego, "Retired Navy. Like [Bernie's] stand on taking care of veterans. If you can't afford to take care of veterans you can't afford to fight a war." And also Jack from Massachusetts, "I'm a disabled Marine combat veteran, Bernie has always supported veterans with deeds and not just hot air. I'd love to have a President like that." I will always fight for Hilary, Peter, and Jack. And if we all stand together, we can protect and strengthen the care we provide for everyone who has served our country. "" The United States has spent trillions of dollars sending our young men and women to fight in Iraq and Afghanistan. Surely we can come together to ensure the Department of Veterans Affairs has the resources needed to care for them when they return. ________________________________________ _**National Journal**_ : "The Christie Comeback Continued in Milwaukee"
* "The New Jersey governor's focus on Clinton, humor won over many Republicans"
* "Chris Christie delivered another strong debate performance Tuesday"

**Former U.S. Senator Scott P. Brown** : "@GovChristie won. Great closing answer." **_National Review 's_ Jay Nordlinger**: "Chris Christie looks both pugnacious and presidential. Pretty hard thing to pull off." **_Cox Radio 's_ Jamie Dupree**: "GOP debate undercard is over. Christie seemed to be the easy winner." **_Daily Caller 's _Matt Lewis**: "Santorum yelled. Jindal complained. Huckabee's wife yells. Christie rocked." **Frank Luntz** : "Looks like @ChrisChristie is doing what Carly Fiorina did so well: Save the attacks for the other party, not your own."
* ".@ChrisChristie gets it: The opponent isn't each other. The opponent is Hillary Clinton. He is nailing this junior #GOPDebate."

**_FiveThirtyEight 's_ Leah Libresco**: "Christie is the strongest, most memorable candidate on stage." **_FiveThirtyEight 's_ Nate Silver**: "To my eyes, Christie was the most polished and tactically sound." **_Fox News ' _Monica Crowley:** @ChrisChristie dominated first debate. And @FoxBusiness ran a supremely professional, substantive debate. Nicely done. **_The Plain Dealer 's_ Henry J. Gomez**: "Chris Christie was very good. If anyone post-Fiorina can turn an undercard win into a bounce (back) to prime time, it's him." **Suzy Welch** : "Solid, serious #GOPDebate, which Christie obviously won." **_The Washington Times ' _Jessica Chasmar**: "Christie was the clear winner of tonight's undercard." **_The Washington Post 's_ Jennifer Rubin**: "Christie killing it with foreign policy knowledge/specifics on China" **_Breitbart:_** "Chris Christie Slams Hillary Clinton in Almost Every GOP Debate Answer" **_New York Post 's_ John Podhoretz**: "It's like batting practice for Christie." **_The Washington Post 's_ Robert Costa**: "Christie is making a play for Trump voters. Tough talk? Check. Aggressive stance on China? Check."
* "So far, undercard giving Christie a showcase. Talking up cops, fiscal issues, China. And sans Trump, lone candidate w/ Northeast bravado."

**_The Washington Post 's_ James Hohmann**: "Christie's line that he's vetoed more tax increases than any governor in US history, citing @GroverNorquist's ATR, was a strong moment." **_U.S. News & World Report's_ David Catanese**: "Wow, Christie passes swiping back at Jindal. Right back to Hillary. Electability again. "I've won in a blue state." **Former GE Chairman and CEO Jack Welch** : ".@GovChristie is doing a fabulous job. Crazy that he is not on main stage sharing his thinking and presence with more." **_The Huffington Post 's _Amanda Terkel**: "Chris Christie didn't get a ton of notice during the main debates in the past. But really dominating the JV one." **_The Washington Post 's_ Ed O'Keefe**: ".@ChrisChristie having a standout performance tonight." **_National Review 's_ Rich Lowry**: "Christie wins exchange w/ Jindal by elevating himself above it" **_CBS News ' _Alex Romano**: "Christie staying above the fray as Jindal tries to continue attack his and Huckabee's records as guvs" **Larry Sabato** : "Christie's target is Hillary Clinton, Jindal's target is the entire GOP field."
* "Easy call: Undercard winner is Christie, loser is Jindal. Argue amongst yourselves about Huckabee and Santorum.

**_National Review 's_ Rich Lowry**: "Christie stands up for cops--good for him. "I'll have your back"
* "Has any GOP candidate as forthrightly stood up for the cops as Christie did right there? Maybe Trump?"

**_Fox News ' _Angela McGlowan**: ".@ChrisChristie is getting a lot of applause as well. He seems to be having a good night" **_Fox Business Network 's_ Charles Gasparino**: "great point on impact of zero i rate policy of the fed by @GovChristie: has led to " more income inequality in this admin than any admin" **Michael Steele** : "Undercard best thing to happen to @ChrisChristie. Right now he's owning the stage despite some heel nibbling by @BobbyJindal" **_CNN 's _Michelle Kosinski**: "Still can't believe Christie isn't in the main debate though. I think he always shines in that setting. Cuts through." **Patrick Ruffini** : "Undercard debate could be good for Christie. Gives him a chance to stand out against a smaller, lower wattage field." **_Politico 's_ Shane Goldmacher:** "Christie keeps pivoting to Clinton. And each time the crowd applauds. This was the Gingrich 2012 tactic. And it worked then." **_Fox News ' _Abby Huntsman**: "Have a feeling this is the last time Chris Christie will be at the kiddie table." **_CBN 's_ David Brody**: ".@ChrisChristie basically saying President Obama doesn't respect the military.Then he dropped the mic." **_The Washington Post 's_ Abby D. Phillip**: "Christie had a strong debate for sure. Jindal's attacks got people's attention, but it's unclear if they got them to like him." **_The Weekly Standard 's_ John McCormack: **"Strong finish from @ChrisChristie."

**_National Journal 's_ Alex Roarty**: My story: He might have been on the undercard debate, but the Christie comeback continued Tuesday _**National Journal 's **_**Josh Kraushaar:** If Jeb doesn't turn things around in primetime, Christie cld be big beneficiary _**Politico** :_ "Christie Punches At Clinton"
* "The New Jersey governor dominates the undercard debate by ignoring his GOP rivals."

name: George Pataki
subject: Honoring Those Who Serve
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pataki for President, Inc
body:
Friend,

On this Veterans Day, I want to take a moment to honor those who
serve our country in uniform. America's veterans put their lives at
risk to keep us safe. For that, they deserve our eternal gratitude.

As a military dad, I've had the privilege of raising two courageous
heroes who wore our nation's uniform. My older son Teddy served as a
Marine officer in Iraq, while my younger son Owen is an Army officer
who recently returned from Afghanistan. I can't tell you how proud I
am that both of my sons--as well as those with whom they
served--answered our nation's call when Islamic terrorism reached our
shores.

Please join me in honoring our veterans, and keeping them in our
thoughts and prayers as we celebrate their service on this Veterans
Days.

Very truly yours,

George Pataki
